scores.correctness_notes: Misses supported bases almalinux@9, ubuntu@24.10, ubuntu@25.04. Critically misses the modern multi-base platform syntax (embedding base in platform key like `ubuntu@22.04:amd64`). Incorrectly frames the legacy `bases` key as the primary multi-base mechanism, when the gold standard shows the modern `platforms` key supports multi-base via embedded base names.
scores.hallucination_notes: `charmcraft pack --base-index N` is not mentioned in gold standard and appears to be fabricated. Calling `bases` key 'deprecated' is an unverified claim not in gold standard.
